title: Improve Transparency and Stability for Families and Children Act
short_title: Improve Transparency and Stability for Families and Children Act
plain_summary: This bill makes new rules about how quickly states must use federal money that helps poor families. States will have to promise to use their money within one year and actually spend it within two years. States can save some money for later, but they cannot save too much. They can only keep up to fifteen percent of their yearly money, and their total savings cannot be more than half of what they got the year before.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Improve Transparency and Stability for Families and Children Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Deadlines for the Obligation and Expenditure of Funds
sections.text: This section changes the rules for how states must use federal money they receive for helping needy families. Under the new rules, when a state gets money for one year, they must promise to use it by the end of the next year. They must actually spend the money by the end of the year after that. For example, if a state gets money in twenty twenty six, they must promise to use it by the end of twenty twenty seven and actually spend it by the end of twenty twenty eight. However, states can save up to fifteen percent of their yearly money for future use. But there is a limit on how much they can save overall. The total amount saved cannot be more than fifty percent of what they got the year before. If a state wants to save some money, they must tell the federal government about their plan by a certain deadline. These new rules will start on October first, twenty twenty six.
